opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I'm 28 weeks pregnant and I haven't felt the baby kick in two hours. I'm absolutely terrified something has gone wrong and I can't stop crying.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: I usually feel the baby moving all the time and tonight it's gone quiet. I have a routine antenatal appointment next week but I couldn't wait — I need to know the baby is okay.

symptom_narrative.onset: about two hours ago this evening
symptom_narrative.duration: two hours of not feeling the usual movements
symptom_narrative.character: the baby normally kicks and moves a lot; tonight I noticed a quiet spell and now I can't stop focusing on it
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the scariest thing I've felt this pregnancy — I'm panicking
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant quiet spell since it started this evening
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: the more I focus on it the more frightened I get
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: I feel panicky, my heart is racing and I've been crying and pacing
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: the baby has been active and moving normally all through the second and third trimester until tonight

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: currently 28 weeks pregnant, on routine antenatal shared care

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives with her partner, who is at home with her and very supportive

objective_data_offered.value: she says she has lain down on her side and had a cold drink and still has not felt the usual movements over roughly the last two hours
on video she looks tearful and anxious, is breathing quickly and keeps shifting position; her partner is beside her
